buckets.description: The compiler records source facts but fails to admit or preserve the executable rule needed for inference.
A source-stated conditional rule is flattened into an already-executed fact.
Permission to do something is treated as proof that the event occurred.
A source-owned claim becomes a supported finding without watch-log, instrument-record, or certified-log support.
Unless/except/override clauses are dropped or applied to the wrong rule.
Glass tide, storm alarm, harbor closure, or revocation windows are missing or conflated.
Numeric thresholds such as pressure/coolant, cargo value, or six-hour test separation are not represented for inference.
Bounded absence such as no emergency signal or no signed clearance is mishandled.
Certified logs, uncertified notes, observations, and claims are not ranked correctly.
Permit authority is incorrectly generalized to repair authority or other roles.
Emergency priority, Treasurer veto, or Harbor Warden override rules are not preserved.
Sacred-cargo exception to salvage reward is lost.
The KB contains support but post-ingestion QA queries the wrong predicate family.
